capabilities.title: Architecture and composition
capabilities.summary: Lifecycle-aware Model, System, Utility, Controller, installer, command, query, and event composition.
capabilities.boundary: Use roles for ownership and lifecycle, not as mandatory wrappers for every class.

capabilities.title: Async lifecycle and cancellation
capabilities.summary: Owned completion, cancellation scopes, signal waits, retries, progress, gates, and bounded aggregation.
capabilities.boundary: The caller owns business retries and rollback; GF owns reusable terminal-state and cancellation mechanics.

capabilities.title: Assets, resource identity, and content
capabilities.summary: Async resource handles, stable resource keys, catalogs, preload plans, and optional content packages.
capabilities.boundary: Projects define content taxonomy and distribution policy; GF provides identity, lifecycle, plans, and diagnostics.

capabilities.title: Configuration and data pipelines
capabilities.summary: Runtime config resources plus staged, validated, versioned-IR table compilation at build time.
capabilities.boundary: Business columns and schemas belong to project inputs; reusable reading, validation, target, and commit mechanics belong to the tool.

capabilities.title: Storage, save graphs, and migration
capabilities.summary: Local storage codecs, backend synchronization, slot metadata, scene save graphs, identity, and explicit migration.
capabilities.boundary: Projects own save fields, compatibility guarantees, conflict policy, and cloud provider adapters.

capabilities.title: Input mapping and rebinding
capabilities.summary: Abstract actions, contexts, modifiers, triggers, recording, conflict analysis, rebinding, touch, and drag/drop mechanisms.
capabilities.boundary: GF maps intent; projects define action names, UI, accessibility policy, and gameplay meaning.

capabilities.title: UI state, binding, and navigation
capabilities.summary: Layered UI navigation, screen state, reactive binding, repeaters, transitions, and focus-friendly project composition.
capabilities.boundary: GF owns navigation and binding mechanics; projects own visual hierarchy, copy, and product flows.

capabilities.title: Audio playback and backend adaptation
capabilities.summary: Audio clips, banks, events, pooled emitters, buses, beat clocks, and optional middleware backends.
capabilities.boundary: Middleware SDK calls belong in a backend adapter; projects own event naming and mix policy.

capabilities.title: State machines and flow orchestration
capabilities.summary: Node and resource state machines, flow graphs, action queues, behavior trees, and decision mechanisms.
capabilities.boundary: Select one mechanism by state ownership and observability; do not layer several orchestrators without a distinct responsibility.

capabilities.title: Diagnostics, logs, support, and operations
capabilities.summary: Structured logs, bounded diagnostics, operation timelines, debug surfaces, support reports, and build metadata.
capabilities.boundary: Collect the minimum needed data, bound all histories, and require explicit consent for precise or private diagnostics.

capabilities.title: Deterministic data and simulation primitives
capabilities.summary: Fixed random sequences, canonical Variant serialization, seeds, tick histories, and explicit deterministic scopes.
capabilities.boundary: Declare the deterministic scope and numeric contract; do not assume all Godot values or physics are cross-platform deterministic.

capabilities.title: Network transport and contracts
capabilities.summary: Replaceable byte transports, channels, typed message contracts, snapshots, dirty state, histories, and session mechanisms.
capabilities.boundary: GF does not choose authority, matchmaking, lobby policy, provider identity, prediction, or rollback for the project.

capabilities.title: Platform and SDK adapters
capabilities.summary: Project-owned translation boundaries for identity, lobby, storage, achievements, social, lifecycle, payment, and other provider capabilities.
capabilities.boundary: Provider code is a separate adapter. Only stable provider-neutral contracts are candidates for GF.

capabilities.title: Reusable domain components
capabilities.summary: Generic inventory, attributes, traits, equipment, quests, level catalogs, interactions, combat, and turn-based primitives.
capabilities.boundary: GF components store generic state and mechanisms; game economy, rewards, balance, and content semantics remain project code.

capabilities.title: Dialogue resources and execution
capabilities.summary: Provider-neutral dialogue resources, conditions, mutations, responses, runners, and strict text compilation.
capabilities.boundary: GF does not define character databases, quest side effects, localization storage, UI, or a project-specific script language.

capabilities.title: Spatial math and physics mechanisms
capabilities.summary: Grid/path math, spatial queries, camera mechanisms, hit detection, buoyancy sampling, and physics-independent policies.
capabilities.boundary: Projects own collision layers, world semantics, probe placement, authority, and force application policy.

capabilities.title: Project layout and authoring tools
capabilities.summary: Optional profile-driven project structure, data compilers, generators, editor contributions, and CI validation.
capabilities.boundary: Tools are optional and profile-driven; they must not turn one game's directory or schema into a GF runtime requirement.
